name: PipeEvent - Pipe Created
definition: Sysmon Event ID 17 records PipeEvent - Pipe Created.
trigger_scenarios: The event is emitted when the configured Sysmon operation occurs.

key_fields.explanation: PipeName \\pipe\\wkssvc, \\pipe\\svcctl, or a rare custom pipe created by a process under C:\\Users\\ can support remote service or C2 investigation.
Use the initiating image and ProcessGuid to join Event ID 1 command-line context.
Use the timestamp to correlate with related activity.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.
detection_notes: PipeName \\pipe\\wkssvc, \\pipe\\svcctl, or a rare custom pipe created by a process under C:\\Users\\ can support remote service or C2 investigation. This is a T1559 Inter-Process Communication signal; preserve the concrete value and correlate the initiating Image, ProcessGuid, and logon context before closing.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 17
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=17 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
